title: Best Restaurants in Bangkok Thailand (2026)
meta_description: A more useful Bangkok restaurant guide for 2026, with stronger picks for rooftop dining, riverside Thai meals, Cantonese splurges, and destination tasting menus.
intro: This Bangkok restaurant guide is meant to be more useful than a generic ranking. Instead of pretending there is one universal best place to eat in a city as big as Bangkok, it focuses on strong picks for different types of meals: rooftop dinners, refined Cantonese cooking, riverside Thai restaurants, and destination tasting-menu addresses.

items.name: SEEN Restaurant & Bar Bangkok
items.description: SEEN is one of Bangkok's stronger rooftop restaurant picks if you want a skyline-and-river setting as much as the meal itself. At Avani+ Riverside, it works best for sunset drinks, a polished dinner, or a more celebratory night out rather than a quiet classic Thai meal.
highlights: Best for dinner or drinks rather than a budget meal
insider_tips: Go close to sunset if the view matters as much as the food.
Treat it as a mood-and-view restaurant, not as the place for the city's most traditional Thai cooking.
items.current_info: Cuisine: Rooftop, international • Best for: sunset drinks and a polished dinner • Good to know: reserve ahead if you care about seating

items.name: Mott 32 Bangkok
items.description: Mott 32 is one of Bangkok's most defensible upscale Chinese picks, especially if you want a polished special-occasion meal in the Mahanakhon building. The menu leans Cantonese, with signature dim sum, barbecue dishes, and Peking duck in a clearly high-end setting.
highlights: Strong option for a splurge dinner
insider_tips: This works better as a planned dinner than as a spontaneous cheap eat after sightseeing.
If you go with a group, order across dim sum and barbecue so the menu makes more sense.
items.current_info: Cuisine: Chinese, contemporary Cantonese • Best for: upscale dinner • Good to know: this is one of the more polished splurge meals in central Bangkok

items.name: Rongros Bangkok
items.description: Rongros is one of the easiest Bangkok restaurant picks to recommend because it pairs recognizably Thai dishes with one of the city's most memorable Wat Arun views. It is visitor-friendly, but the riverside setting and the cooking together make it more than just a photo stop.
items.location: Maha Rat Road, Tha Tien / riverside
highlights: Thai classics in a polished setting
Best with a reservation around sunset
insider_tips: Book ahead if you want the stronger view-facing tables near sunset.
This is better for a sit-down riverside meal than for a quick budget lunch.
items.current_info: Cuisine: Thai • Best for: riverside lunch or sunset dinner • Good to know: location is a major part of the appeal

items.name: Supanniga Eating Room Tha Tien
items.description: Supanniga Eating Room Tha Tien is a better fit if you want homestyle Thai cooking with river views rather than a heavier tasting-menu format. The kitchen draws on family recipes and eastern Thai flavors, which gives the menu more identity than a generic tourist-facing Thai restaurant.
items.location: Maha Rat Road, Tha Tien / riverside
insider_tips: This is a better recommendation for travelers who want Thai flavors with a clear point of view rather than a generic upscale menu.
Lunch can be the easier option if you want the area without prime-time dinner crowds.
items.current_info: Cuisine: Thai, eastern Thai • Best for: river-view Thai meal • Good to know: more grounded and homestyle than many tourist-heavy riverside spots

items.name: Gaa
items.description: Gaa belongs on a serious Bangkok restaurant list because it is one of the city's clearest destination-dining addresses for travelers who want a planned tasting-menu meal. Chef Garima Arora's cooking draws on Indian roots and Thai produce in a way that feels personal and distinctly Bangkok rather than interchangeable luxury dining.
highlights: Best for a planned splurge rather than a casual walk-in
insider_tips: This is a destination meal that needs planning and reservation lead time.
Book it only if you actively want the tasting-menu format; it is not a casual neighborhood dinner.
items.current_info: Cuisine: Indian, contemporary • Best for: destination tasting menu • Good to know: one of Bangkok's clearest splurge restaurants

items.name: Kodtalay The Riverfront Seafood Buffet
items.description: Kodtalay The Riverfront Seafood Buffet is a 4.9-rated Seafood, Asian restaurant in Bangkok. Known for Grilled prawns, Crab curry, Oysters.
items.current_info: Cuisine: Seafood, Asian • Price: $$ - $$$ • Rating: 4.9/5 • 314 reviews

items.name: Tossakan Thai Curry House
items.description: Tossakan Thai Curry House is a 4.7-rated Thai restaurant in Bangkok. Known for Beef green curry, Massoman curry.

items.name: Yao Restaurant and Rooftop Bar
items.description: Yao Restaurant and Rooftop Bar is a 4.8-rated Chinese, Asian restaurant in Bangkok. Known for Peking duck, Fantastic view.
items.current_info: Cuisine: Chinese, Asian • Price: $$ - $$$ • Rating: 4.8/5 • 1.853 reviews

items.name: Lamphu Tree Restaurant
items.description: Lamphu Tree Restaurant is a 4.9-rated Asian, Thai restaurant in Bangkok. Known for Grilled vegetables, Green curry rice, Pad Thai.
items.current_info: Cuisine: Asian, Thai • Price: $ • Rating: 4.9/5 • 240 reviews

items.name: Market Café
items.description: Market Café is a 4.9-rated Asian, Thai restaurant in Bangkok. Known for Pad Thai, Great service, Great atmosphere.
items.current_info: Cuisine: Asian, Thai • Price: $$ - $$$ • Rating: 4.9/5 • 905 reviews
